title: Global Peace Processes Update — Apr 29, 2026
summary: Iran submitted a new de-escalation proposal on April 28 through Pakistan, offering to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for lifting the US naval blockade while deferring nuclear talks — but the US expressed skepticism and Trump warned Iran 'better get smart soon' on April 29, with analysts citing a growing frozen conflict risk as both Islamabad rounds have collapsed. The UN's ASG Khiari told the Security Council on April 28 that Gaza's ceasefire is 'increasingly fragile,' with 800+ Palestinians killed since the ceasefire began and 1.8 million displaced. In a positive development, the DRC National Assembly approved Washington Accords ratification bills on April 28, sending them to the Senate. Events, KPIs, map points, and contested claims updated to reflect April 28–29 developments.

title: Global Peace Processes Tracker Update — Tue Apr 28, 2026
summary: Iran Submits De-escalation Proposal Via Pakistan: Reopen Strait of Hormuz in Exchange for Lifting Naval Blockade; Nuclear Talks Deferred. UN Tells Security Council Gaza Ceasefire 'Increasingly Fragile'; 800+ Palestinians Killed Since Ceasefire Began, 1.8M Displaced. DRC National Assembly Adopts Washington Accords Ratification Bills; Sends to Senate — Positive Step for DRC–Rwanda Peace Process.

title: Global Peace Processes Update — Apr 27, 2026
summary: Trump cancelled the Witkoff and Kushner Pakistan trip on April 26, collapsing Iran nuclear Round 2 before it began — both Islamabad rounds have now failed with the 20-year vs. 5-year enrichment gap unresolved, and Iranian FM Araghchi pressed on with a regional tour preserving back-channel architecture. Israel issued forced evacuation orders for seven southern Lebanon towns on April 26 and continued airstrikes under the nominally extended ceasefire, putting the Lebanon–Israel framework under acute strain with 2,509 killed since March 2. The UN Security Council called on April 27 for consolidation of the Gaza Phase 2 ceasefire as Hamas rejected the US disarmament clause. Ceasefires in force fell to 10 globally. KPIs, map points, contested claims, and political actor roles updated to reflect the April 26–27 diplomatic deterioration.

title: Global Peace Processes Tracker Update — Sun Apr 26, 2026
summary: On April 26, 2026, President Trump abruptly cancelled the planned trip of US Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and adviser Jared Kushner to Pakistan for a second round of Iran nuclear talks. Israel Issues Forced Evacuation Orders for Seven South Lebanon Towns; Ceasefire Framework Under Acute Strain.

title: Global Peace Processes Update — Apr 25, 2026
summary: US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner flew to Islamabad on April 25 for a potential second round of Iran nuclear talks, as Iranian FM Araghchi arrived in Pakistan via intermediary channel — Tehran publicly denied any direct US–Iran meeting was planned, maintaining calculated diplomatic ambiguity while the 20-year vs 5-year enrichment-pause gap remains unresolved. The Lebanon–Israel ceasefire was extended 3 weeks on April 23, but Israel continued operations in southern Lebanon on April 24 and Hezbollah declared the extension 'meaningless,' raising structural questions about the framework's durability without Hezbollah participation. The DRC–Rwanda 5th Joint Oversight Committee met in Washington on April 23, with US officials characterizing Rwanda's compliance with the December 2025 Washington Accords as 'deeply disappointing.' KPIs, map points, contested claims, and political actors were updated across all active processes.

title: Global Peace Processes Tracker Update — Fri Apr 24, 2026
summary: Lebanon–Israel 3-Week Ceasefire Extension Holds Under Stress; Israel Continues Operations in South Lebanon. DRC–Rwanda 5th Joint Oversight Committee Meets in Washington; US Presses Rwanda on Compliance Failures.

title: Global Peace Processes Update — Apr 23, 2026
summary: Lebanon and Israel held a second round of direct talks in Washington on April 23 — described as the first direct Lebanon-Israel diplomatic contact in approximately three decades — with Lebanese and Israeli ambassadors meeting before Secretary Rubio, seeking a one-month extension of the 10-day ceasefire expiring April 26. Lebanese President Aoun called it 'an opportunity that may not arise again,' demanding Israeli troop withdrawal, return of detainees, and reconstruction support, while Hezbollah condemned Lebanon's participation. Simultaneously, the US military seized an Iranian oil tanker in the Indian Ocean, one day after Iran seized two commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz; Iran's parliament speaker warned the Strait will remain closed as long as the US naval blockade continues, calling it a ceasefire violation. US-Iran talks remain frozen with VP Vance's Pakistan trip called off. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun was added to the political section; the contested claim on coercive diplomacy and the Washington DC map point were updated to reflect April 23 developments.

title: Global Peace Processes Update — Apr 22, 2026
summary: Trump indefinitely extended the US–Iran ceasefire on April 21, contingent on Iran submitting a unified proposal, but on April 22 the IRGC seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz and the US issued a 3-to-5 day implicit ultimatum — with the nuclear gap stark (US demands 20-yr pause, Iran offers 5 yrs). Lebanon's 10-day US-brokered ceasefire, begun April 16, is up for a one-month extension at State Department talks with Secretary Rubio joining, as Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam demands full Israeli withdrawal. Myanmar's junta 100-day peace talks offer was rejected by the National Unity Government and resistance forces. Map points were added for Beirut (Lebanon ceasefire) and Naypyidaw (Myanmar spoiler). Two new political figures added: Secretary Rubio and Lebanese PM Salam.

title: Global Peace Processes Update — Apr 21, 2026
summary: The 14-day US–Iran ceasefire expired on April 21 as VP JD Vance and Envoy Steve Witkoff flew to Islamabad for a second round of talks — in a last-minute reversal, Iran sent a delegation despite having publicly declared on April 20 it had 'no plans to attend.' Trump raised the stakes by threatening to resume military operations if no agreement was reached. The DRC–M23 Ceasefire Oversight and Verification Mechanism (COVM) launched initial verification missions following the April 18 joint statement operationalizing the EJVM+, with prisoner releases due by April 28. The political section was updated to include VP Vance as a key actor in Iran diplomacy, and the contested claim on coercive diplomacy was updated to reflect the nuanced April 21 reversal.

title: Global Peace Processes Update — Apr 20, 2026
summary: US-Iran nuclear diplomacy collapsed on April 20 after Iran declared no plans for further negotiations, following a US naval blockade and cargo ship seizure that followed the breakdown of Islamabad talks. Sudan's civil war marked its third anniversary on April 15 with 150,000+ killed and 14 million displaced; the US imposed new sanctions and presented a five-pillar peace roadmap through envoy Massad Boulos, though SAF and RSF remain far apart. The UN Security Council warned that the DRC-Rwanda Washington Accords are stalling, with Rwandan troops still in eastern DRC and M23 holding Goma. In Ukraine, Russia signaled rejection of Kyiv's 20-point counterproposal while Zelensky demanded inclusion in US-Russia talks; a 175-soldier Easter POW exchange offered a rare humanitarian bright spot. Colombia's ELN peace process remains frozen 15+ months after the Catatumbo massacre, with the May 31 presidential election creating uncertainty about the Total Peace framework's future.

summary: Russia Signals Rejection of Ukraine's 20-Point Peace Counterproposal.
